topic: Tool use methods — function calling, schemas, and execution patterns

sources.title: Anthropic: Tool use with Claude (overview)
keyInsights: Canonical tool_use/tool_result block model with stop_reason='tool_use'
Client tools run in your application; server tools run on Anthropic infrastructure
Tool system prompt adds fixed 313-346 tokens overhead on Claude 4.x models
If required parameter missing, Opus asks; Sonnet/Haiku may guess

sources.title: Anthropic: Define tools
keyInsights: Tool description is by far the most important factor in performance; aim for 3-4 sentences
input_examples (schema-validated) help complex tools; adds 20-200 tokens
tool_choice: auto|any|tool|none — any/tool prefills assistant turn, no preface text
Extended thinking incompatible with tool_choice any or tool (400 error)

sources.title: Anthropic: Strict tool use
keyInsights: strict: true uses grammar-constrained sampling to guarantee schema conformance
Requires additionalProperties: false; schemas cached up to 24 hours
Tool name also guaranteed valid under strict mode
HIPAA warning: never put PHI in schema property names, enum values, patterns

sources.title: Anthropic: Parallel tool use
keyInsights: Parallel tool calls are default on Claude 4.x; disable with disable_parallel_tool_use
CRITICAL: return all tool_results in a single user message or model stops parallelizing
System prompt phrasing affects parallelism: 'invoke all relevant tools simultaneously'
Track avg_tools_per_message > 1.0 to verify parallel calls work

sources.title: Anthropic: Fine-grained tool streaming
keyInsights: Set eager_input_streaming: true per-tool to disable buffering/validation
Reduces latency ~15s → ~3s for large parameter payloads
May yield invalid/partial JSON if max_tokens hits mid-parameter
Wrap invalid JSON as {'INVALID_JSON': '...'} when feeding back to model

sources.title: Anthropic: Tool search tool
keyInsights: Multi-server MCP setup can consume 55k tokens in tool definitions before work starts
Selection accuracy degrades above 30-50 available tools
defer_loading: true keeps tools out of system prompt; loaded via regex or BM25 search
Preserves prompt caching; supports up to 10,000 tools; 3-5 results per search

sources.title: Anthropic Engineering: Writing tools for agents
keyInsights: Consolidate: one schedule_event tool beats list_users + list_events + create_event
Avoid low-signal tools that force agents to process irrelevant data
Namespace tool names by service: github_list_prs, slack_send_message
Return semantic IDs (names, slugs) not UUIDs; agents reason better on language

sources.title: Anthropic Engineering: Effective context engineering
keyInsights: Just-in-time retrieval: load info on demand, don't pre-load everything
Context has an 'attention budget' — performance degrades with too many tokens
Tools should be self-contained, robust to error, extremely clear in intent
Compaction + structured note-taking + multi-agent for long-horizon tasks

sources.title: OpenAI Cookbook: Function calling with chat models
keyInsights: tools array format: {type: 'function', function: {name, description, parameters}}
tool_choice: omit (auto) | 'none' | {type: 'function', function: {name}} | 'required'
Parallel tool calls supported on GPT-4o+; finish_reason = 'tool_calls'
function.arguments is a JSON string — always JSON.parse before use

sources.title: OpenAI: Introducing Structured Outputs in the API
keyInsights: strict: true uses grammar-based token filtering for 100% schema conformance
First requests preprocess schema into context-free grammar (latency cost)
Only JSON Schema subset supported; all objects need additionalProperties: false
Edge case: confused model may loop generating valid output until max_tokens

sources.title: Simon Willison: OpenAI Structured Outputs analysis
keyInsights: Technique borrowed from jsonformer and llama.cpp
OpenAI engineer recommendation: 'in 99% of cases, you'll want strict: true'
OpenAI strict mode eliminates optional properties — use unions with null instead
gpt-4o-2024-08-06 also delivered 50% input cost cut + doubled output tokens

sources.title: Model Context Protocol: Introduction
keyInsights: MCP = 'USB-C for AI applications' — standardized context + tool exchange
Supported by Claude, ChatGPT, VS Code, Cursor, MCPJam, and others
Open protocol — build once, integrate everywhere
Distinguishes connecting data sources, tools, and workflows

sources.title: MCP: Architecture overview
keyInsights: Host → Client → Server model over JSON-RPC 2.0
Three server primitives: tools, resources, prompts
Three client primitives: sampling, elicitation, logging
Transports: stdio (local) or Streamable HTTP (remote, with optional SSE)

sources.title: MCP Specification 2025-06-18
keyInsights: MCP inspired by Language Server Protocol — standardize across ecosystem
Tools MUST be treated as arbitrary code execution; descriptions untrusted
Hosts MUST obtain user consent before invoking tools or exposing data
Sampling requests MUST be explicitly approved by user

sources.title: MCP Reference Server Implementations
keyInsights: Reference servers: filesystem, git, fetch, memory, sequential-thinking, time, everything
SDKs for TypeScript, Python, C#, Go, Java, Kotlin, PHP, Ruby, Rust, Swift
Memory server implements knowledge-graph-based persistence
Launched via npx or uvx for local stdio transport

sources.title: Hugging Face: Tool use in Transformers
keyInsights: apply_chat_template accepts Python functions or JSON schemas directly
Google-style docstrings required for auto JSON-schema generation via get_json_schema
Tool calls go in tool_calls key of assistant message (dict, not JSON string)
Hermes-2-Pro, Command-R, Mixtral-8x22B support tool use; formats are model-specific

sources.title: Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard (BFCL)
keyInsights: v1: AST evaluation; v2: enterprise APIs; v3: multi-turn; v4: agentic with web search
Two pathways: FC (native function calling) and Prompt (text workarounds)
Metrics: accuracy, latency (seconds), estimated USD cost
Scoring is unweighted average across sub-categories

sources.title: BFCL v3: Multi-turn function calling
keyInsights: 1,000 entries: Base Multi-Turn + Missing Params/Functions + Long-Context + Composite
State-based evaluation compares final backend state to ground truth
Response-based evaluation uses subset-matching of function calls
Three failure modes: Implicit Action Gaps, State Unawareness, Over-Planning

sources.title: ToolLLM / ToolBench (arXiv 2307.16789)
keyInsights: 16,464 real-world RESTful APIs across 49 categories from RapidAPI Hub
DFS-based decision tree improves reasoning by exploring multiple paths
ToolLLaMA achieves comparable performance to ChatGPT on API calling
Strong zero-shot generalization to unseen APIs (APIBench OOD)

sources.title: Gorilla: LLM Connected with Massive APIs (arXiv 2305.15334)
keyInsights: Even GPT-4 struggles with accurate API input arguments and hallucinates usage
Retrieval-aware training substantially mitigates hallucination
Adapts to test-time document changes — no retraining for API updates
APIBench: HuggingFace + TorchHub + TensorHub evaluation dataset
